title: Tapping to Give Freely Without Fear
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the fear that generosity will leave you short
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 58 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the hesitation that appears before giving money, time, or support — the worry that generosity drains rather than expands. The reframe moves toward giving as a natural, joyful act that stays in flow with receiving.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 58 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to give to someone and notice a pull of reluctance, or when generosity feels like it costs you something you can't afford.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 58 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the fear-of-depletion pattern that sits beneath held-back generosity: the sense that money given away is money gone, that the return is uncertain, that giving from a small pile is risky. The reframe moves toward giving as part of the same circulation that brings money in.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to worry that giving would leave me short, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though generosity sometimes felt unsafe, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feared I wouldn't have enough if I gave, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I sometimes held back from sharing.
Side of eye: I wondered if I'd regret it.
Under eye: I thought generosity might drain me.
Under nose: I feared money wouldn't return.
Chin: I second-guessed each gift.
Collarbone: I made giving heavy.
Under arm: I'm ready to change that.
Top of head: I want giving to feel joyful and safe.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Giving flows back multiplied.
Side of eye: I love blessing others with ease.
Under eye: I feel richer as I share.
Under nose: I trust money to refill quickly.
Chin: I give with a light, open heart.
Collarbone: I know generosity is part of my wealth.
Under arm: I love creating ripple effects of good.
Top of head: I give freely and stay abundant.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 58 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
